title: rosa stylosa subsp. cantabrica
intro: Rosa stylosa subsp. cantabrica, also known as the Cantabrian rose or Rosa cantabrica, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. The health benefits associated with Rosa stylosa subsp. cantabrica include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations, among other uses. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, or used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves for various health benefits and therapeutic applications.
